name: NanoGPT

dataResidency.euRegionDetails: NanoGPT states that prompts are passed directly to the relevant third-party service provider and that providers operate their own infrastructure. It also states AWS S3 is used for cloud storage of media and user-uploaded content. No EU-only processing guarantee or EU region routing commitment was found in the provider's primary documentation.

dataUsage.retentionPolicy: By default, NanoGPT says it does not store prompt or conversation content on its servers. If optional features such as conversation sync or sharing are enabled, it stores the necessary encrypted content. For the OpenAI-compatible Responses API, storage defaults to enabled and request/response data is stored encrypted for up to 7 days unless the store parameter is set to false; the retention period is configurable and data is automatically deleted after expiration. NanoGPT also says it retains personal information only as long as necessary to provide the services or comply with legal obligations.
dataUsage.details: NanoGPT says prompts are sent directly to the selected model provider, does not pass user ID or IP address directly to the end model provider, and warns that underlying providers may store or process prompts under their own policies. It says it enforces the strictest retention/deletion settings each provider makes available, but cannot independently guarantee provider-side non-retention.

notes: NanoGPT publishes a detailed privacy policy and privacy explainer, but no primary-source DPA/AVV, SCC commitment, public sub-processor list, or confirmed security certification was found. The company states it is U.S.-incorporated, uses third-party model providers for inference, and does not offer a documented EU-only processing guarantee; prompts may therefore be processed outside the EU depending on the selected provider.
